intro: Melchiora mannii is a plant species native to the tropical forests of West Africa, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Melchiora mannii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, such as mangiferin, kaempferol, and quercetin, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
